content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã and an integer KKK. Find the number of contiguous subsequences AL,AL+1,‚Ä¶,ARA_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots, A_RAL‚Äã,AL+1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AR‚Äã such that R‚àíL+1‚â•KR-L+1 \ge KR‚àíL+1‚â•K and the KKK-th element of the subsequence (AL+K‚àí1A_{L+K-1}AL+K‚àí1‚Äã) is equal to the maximum of all elements of the entire sequence.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer --- the number of contiguous subsequences satisfying the given conditions.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5‚ãÖ1055 \cdot 10^55‚ãÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: (3,4,5)(3, 4, 5)(3,4,5) is the only contiguous subsequence such that its 333-rd element is equal to the maximum of the whole sequence (which is 555).
